content.text:
## Target's compacted history
The target's earlier work has been folded into the `<summary>` block(s) below — this is what the target itself sees at the head of its context, NOT verbatim transcript. Use it to understand the arc: what was decided, what constraints were set, what was already done. If the window below shows the target acting on an assumption that CONTRADICTS a summary, re-doing work a summary says was completed, or ignoring a constraint a summary records — that is context drift; flag it. Do NOT trace values inside the summaries (they're compacted, not tool-verifiable). Do NOT emit `msg_idx` pointing into this section — `msg_idx` is window-relative. If a summary's paraphrase is ambiguous on the point you're checking, `query_target_history(summary=<id>, question=…)` fetches the original behind it — use sparingly.

<summary id=bznzkr2i>
Faber2026 handoff audit → skill publish → observations.tex conflict review → zach C2D4 beta-table promotion (2026-07-08/09)

## Context and repo state
The user (Jakob Faber) asked to review a Codex handoff and determine what still needs doing on the Faber2026 ApJ manuscript repo (`/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026`, 12 FRBs co-detected CHIME/DSA-110). Handoff path first given as `/var/folders/.../faber2026_task_status_handoff_20260708.md` (not accessible), then USER moved it: <human>I moved the handoff to `~/handoffs/faber2026_task_status_handoff_20260708.md`</human> — "I moved the handoff to `~/handoffs/faber2026_task_status_handoff_20260708.md`". Session was reset once (kernels cleared, artifacts preserved).

Host grants (all rw): the Faber2026 repo, dsa110-FLITS, coherent-fold, `/Users/jakobfaber/handoffs`, `/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/scratch`. Sandbox has **coarse git protection**: `.git` writes denied in all writable locations, `git init/clone` blocked (too many nested repos under the grants). No LaTeX toolchain works in-sandbox. No conda/flits env visible from sandbox; the pipeline ships a uv `.venv` whose python symlink is execution-blocked ("Operation not permitted").

## Task-status handoff read (e10) and live-state reconciliation
The task-status handoff (6559 bytes) claimed: B7/table-emitter lane was dirty-in-progress; `beta_campaign_verdicts.json` had stale suffixes (`casey _sharedzeta`, `hamilton _sharedzeta`, `wilhelm _sharedzeta`, `zach _C1D1`); B3 (exact DSA trial count), B5 (data availability), co-authors/acks still open; recommended `dirty-git-state` skill.

Live git state (e12) contradicted parts: HEAD was `f97addb` (#36 "state foreground apertures as virial criteria (referee minor-7)"), with `#35` (eaeab72, table-emitter bump), `#32` (95c3a4e, DM_MW 95→97) already merged. The B7/table lane the handoff called "dirty" had **landed as PRs**. But the working tree showed `UU sections/observations.tex` — a **stuck interactive rebase** (e14: `interactive rebase in progress; onto f97addb`, rebasing branch `main`, one commit done `834e810`, two remaining `9c161af`/`6fe5e90`). Conflict markers at lines 113/138/173. Local `main` = ORIG_HEAD `84673e134f01013e30f9528ca0cf59336d002acf` (unpushed, 1 ahead of origin). Pipeline submodule dirty at `386e886f373a117e2ce112d69a83565819fd7f59-dirty`.

Read referee-mechanical handoff (e16) and B7-aperture-decision memo (e26 area): documented PR #29 (zach C2D4 panel), #32, #36; owner granted standing push/PR authorization; commits built via isolated `GIT_INDEX_FILE`+`git commit-tree` because `.git` writes blocked; contact email `Jakob Faber <jfaber@caltech.edu>`.

The agent delivered a full assessment (e25): rebase conflict is top blocker; still-open items verified — `beta_campaign_verdicts.json` stale suffixes present, B3 `toa.tex:73` still "$10^{2}$–$10^{3}$", B5 `main.tex:85` TODO, co-authors `auth.tex:6` TODO, acks `main.tex:76` TODO. Noted `dirty-git-state` not in catalog (only `resolving-merge-conflicts` exists).

## dirty-git-state skill: fetched and published
USER (e30): "You can find the dirty-git-state skill in my personal skill/plugin registry: https://github.com/jakobtfaber/my-skillset". Clone blocked twice (coarse git); fetched via GitHub API tarball (`api.github.com/repos/jakobtfaber/my-skillset/tarball/main`) into `/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/scratch/my-skillset`. Skill at `skills/git/dirty-git-state/SKILL.md` (3101 bytes) + `agents/openai.yaml` (cross-platform sidecar, unused by Claude Science). Skill content = "Core Rule: treat dirty files as owned until proven otherwise; pause before clean/stage/commit/revert" + 10-step algorithm (e40).

USER (e43): "Publish the skill in Claude Science please. Do this yourself." Loaded `customize` skill (e45, full `host.skills.*`/`host.agents.*` API). Control-plane repl couldn't read the granted host path directly (PermissionError); staged file into workspace `handoff/dirty-git-state-SKILL.md` via bash, then `host.skills.edit("dirty-git-state","SKILL.md",...)` + `host.skills.publish(...)`. **Published successfully**: `skill_id=skill_01XpWtn7s2s2uJv16RphMJLc`, origin `personal` (e55).

## observations.tex conflict → already resolved concurrently
USER (e50): "Then, lay out the two observations.tex versions." Agent discovered (e57/e59) the conflict markers were **gone**, rebase no longer in progress, HEAD advanced to `765a40a` (then later `4a00aa0`), new doc `docs/rse/specs/handoff-2026-07-08-18-12-b7-cgm-census-resolved.md` present, stray `.REPRODUCE.md.swp` present. The repo is being edited **concurrently by another session**. The resolved-handoff (e61) documented: a concurrent session finished the rebase at 17:41, resolved the conflict as a synthesis keeping the CGM-intersection census (Version B) structure + #36's cluster-mass-provenance sentence, then corrected zach's pierced range in `765a40a`.

Agent laid out both original versions from git (e63/e65): **Version A (PR #36, f97addb)** claimed all 14 confirmed halos within R_vir including the 243 kpc one (logM_halo≳12.3 argument), one paragraph. **Version B (84673e1)** split 14 into 11 pierced + 3 grazing (all toward FRB 20230307A, b/R_vir≈1.05–1.7), added Taylor2011/Cluver2014/DuttonMaccio2014 citations and a mass-indeterminate paragraph (7 WISE-only candidates). The merge (`765a40a`) kept B's structure and corrected pierced range to **b≈102–237 kpc (b/R_vir≈0.15–0.88)**, grazing **b≈122–243 kpc**. Agent flagged: #36's published prose ("each within R_vir") now contradicts the manuscript; if the referee-response letter quotes it, update to `765a40a` (this is Action Item #3 in the resolved handoff).

## (a)(b)(c) triage
USER (e68): "Both (a) and (b), yes, and then (c) after that" — meaning (a) check referee letter, (b) trash swap file, (c) beta_campaign_verdicts.json cleanup.

**(a)**: No referee-response letter exists in the repo (only `docs/referee_report_2026-07-07.md`, the report). Nothing to fix.

**(b)**: `.REPRODUCE.md.swp` already gone (removed by concurrent editing); nothing to trash.

**Auditor finding (e93)** flagged the agent mislabeled the aperture item as "minor-7". Agent verified (e95): item 7 "Aperture values" sits at line 86 under `## Foreground census (§Obs-FG) — non-blocking but important` (heading line 83), ABOVE `## Minor` (line 91). Corrected label: it is **§Obs-FG comment 7**, not minor-7. The "minor-7" label in the handoff and PR #36 is itself inaccurate. Conclusion unchanged: whenever the letter is written, answer §Obs-FG-7 with corrected census numbers.

## (c) zach C2D4 promotion — the main investigation
Traced the mechanism thoroughly. `beta_campaign_verdicts.json` = `{runs_root, missing, rows}`, 12 burst rows each with a `suffix`. `plot_jointmodel_pair.py` filters `final != "FAIL"`, builds NPZ path `runs/data/joint/{burst}_jointmodel{suffix}.npz`, writes to **gitignored** `figures/prototypes/jointmodel_pair/`, and **raises FileNotFoundError if NPZ missing** (never silently emits stale). `data/joint/` is **empty**, so regen fails loud regardless.

Ground truth established (e85/e89): only **zach** is genuinely stale. `johndoeII` already `_C2D2` (correct, promoted in commit `826ba36`). `casey/hamilton/wilhelm _sharedzeta` are current products (hamilton/wilhelm still diagnostic-only, no promoted replacement) — NOT stale; the task-status handoff overstated it. The promoted **zach C2D4 panel already exists tracked** in `figures/jointmodel_pair/` (PR #29 `96a6f72`) with fit artifact `figures/jointmodel_pair/fit_artifacts/zach_jointmodel_C2D4_cwin_nlive160.npz` (scalars α=4.0, β=3.9897163768762995, tau_1ghz=0.1864021408460393, chi2C=1.3468341800327501, chi2D=1.0223746479745357, nC=2, nD=4).

Found: JSON says zach `_C1D1`; script `KNOWN_MULTIPLICITY_FLAGS["zach"]` also stale ("C1D1 misses trailing DSA structure"), contradicting retired-flag docs.

**First ask_user (e91→e97)**: user chose **"Fix zach + also stage the C2D4 NPZ so regen works."** But deeper tracing (e100) revealed zach IS in the manuscript β-table (`tab:beta`) as **FRB 20220207C** — the `_C1D1` verdicts row feeds `export_beta_table.py` → `beta_table_rows.tex`. Swapping the suffix would break the CXD map (KeyError) and rewrite the published table row. Also found the refit-area `zach_jointmodel_C2D4_cwin.npz` is a DIFFERENT earlier run (α=4.021, β=3.979, different modelD checksum) than the tracked `_nlive160` one — the tracked artifact is authoritative.

**Second ask_user (e98→e97, "Revised zach plan")**: user chose **"Full C2D4 promotion into the table (re-grade + restage)"** — treat C2D4 as canonical for FRB 20220207C, accept changed tab:beta numbers.

Then delegation was turned ON and USER's final instruction arrived: **"reduce the cognitive load here, proceed autonomously."**

## Work executed in this span (edits made)
Agent registered pipeline as env `flits-pipeline-dev` (failed — blocked venv python), then installed `emcee`, `pyyaml`, `astropy` into the `python` env and ran the **real** gate functions via importlib (`gate_joint_committed.gate_one` + `grade_beta_campaign.classify_rail`) against the tracked C2D4 artifacts. Computed authoritative zach row: **final=MARGINAL, rail_class=railed-hi (α=4 as limit), alpha=4.0, beta=3.9897163768762995, tau=0.1864021408460393, chi2_chime=1.3468341800327501, chi2_dsa=1.0223746479745357, suffix=_C2D4_cwin, beta_err=[0.006158414817557301, 0.006970146160249158], log_evidence=67983.26603602848, ncall=337976, reason="alpha prior-railed (within 0.1 of bound) -> unconstrained"** (saved to `handoff/zach_c2d4_row.json`). Same citable class as the C1D1 it replaces.

Four zach-scoped edits made (matching johndoeII precedent `826ba36`):
1. `pipeline/analysis/beta_campaign/beta_campaign_verdicts.json` — zach row spliced (12-line diff, all other 11 rows byte-identical). Old zach: suffix=_C1D1, tau=0.2940239093266775, chi2=2.5131695088139154/1.3084150038112266, beta=3.9898946227484458.
2. `pipeline/analysis/beta_campaign/beta_campaign_verdicts.md` — zach row re-rendered (1-line diff): `| zach | C2D4_cwin | 3.990 (+0.007/-0.006) | 4.00 | 0.1864 | 1.35/1.02 | railed-hi | MARGINAL | ... |`.
3. `pipeline/analysis/beta_campaign/beta_table_rows.tex` — regenerated (1-line diff): `FRB 20220207C & $2\times4$ & $\to 4$\tablenotemark{d} & $4$ (limit) & $0.186$ & 1.35/1.02 \\` (was `$1\times1$ ... $0.294$ & 2.51/1.31`). All other 10 rows + chromatica-excluded line identical.
4. `pipeline/analysis/beta_campaign/export_beta_table.py` — added `"_C2D4_cwin": r"$2\times4$"` to CXD map (prevents KeyError). Note: zach is railed-hi so `_alpha_cell` returns "$4$ (limit)" without reading any fit JSON — no data/joint file needed for zach.

Plus separately: removed the retired zach entry from `KNOWN_MULTIPLICITY_FLAGS` in `pipeline/analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/plot_jointmodel_pair.py`.

Full export verified by running with committed interior fit JSONs (freya `_sharedzeta`, phineas `_C3D3`) staged into a temp RUNS dir — produced 11 rows, 1 excluded (chromatica gate FAIL L2 chi2_C=11.59), rc=0.

Saved change-record artifact **`zach_c2d4_promotion_record.md`, artifact_id=e2fbc5b3-94e7-4d71-a7cc-8f2d2475ffca, version_id=69961b8c-62c0-44ec-9bec-f611fe4077b1**.

## Open threads (in progress at chunk end)
Agent was auditing four MORE downstream consumers still carrying zach C1D1 (johndoeII commit `826ba36` had touched all of them):
- `CAMPAIGN_REPORT.md:51` — narrative, `| zach | 3.990 | (4.0 limit) | 0.294 | 2.51/1.31 | railed-hi | MARGINAL |`; also line 94 `| zach | 37.6 | different_screens |`; NOT script-generated.
- `fleet_status.json` — zach block `{"burst":"zach","suffix":"_C1D1",...,"tau_1ghz":0.2940239093266775}`, written by `run_fleet.py`.
- `two_screen_consistency.json/.md` — written by `two_screen.py` (reads `RUNS/data/joint`, may hold DERIVED two-screen numbers; no top-level "zach" key — nested); the `different_screens`/37.6 value likely lives here.
- `citable_alpha_roster.json:176` — zach block still model=C1D1, tau/chi2=C1D1 values, `fit_json` → `analysis/beta_campaign/fits/zach_joint_fit_C1D1.json`; written by `relock_roster.py`.

**Contradiction flagged for author**: `citable_alpha_roster.json` zach block carries `"excluded_from_tab_beta": "Pass-2 fixed-s2 multiplicity (C2D3 vs C2D2) not robust (CONTEXT.md 2026-06-27); budget-tau member only"` — asserts zach should be EXCLUDED from tab:beta, yet the export emits a zach row. Agent's autonomous ruling: user's "full promotion into the table" choice settles this (zach IS in tab:beta); the stale exclusion note gets reconciled, not treated as blocker. Agent committed to: update pure-mirror files (CAMPAIGN_REPORT, fleet_status, citable_alpha_roster) with authoritative C2D4 scalars; recompute two_screen if generator runs here, else flag precisely — no fabricated derived numbers. Was inspecting `two_screen.py` inputs when chunk ended.

Nothing has been staged or committed (`.git` writes blocked anyway; pre-existing dirty lane kept separate). Concurrent editing of the repo by another session is ongoing.

I also appended the journal entry, rebaked the readiness board, and ran `agent-closeout-check`; it passed. The repo remains dirty only with the pre-existi…(796c total)
